name: Vanessa
uniqueMechanic: Ammo system — weapons require ammunition that depletes mid-fight and fully refills after each battle. Aquatic items form a distinct poison/haste loop.
playstyle: The most beginner-friendly hero. Vanessa excels at early aggression with weapons and can pivot into an Aquatic poison loop for late-game scaling. Once you commit to Weapons or Aquatic, pivoting mid-run is extremely difficult.
tips: Buy ammo-free items early to maintain pressure before Day 3.
Pufferfish is your best friend — it poisons every fight passively.
Tortuga gives 1-second Haste to ALL items — pairs with any board.
Flagship was buffed to 5s CD in Season 14; it's viable again.
counters: Freeze-heavy boards lock your weapons mid-charge
High shield boards can weather your early burst
strongAgainst: Boards with few defensive items early
lore: Pirate from the island nation of Pelagos, gathering resources to fight a tyrant who seized her homeland.

name: Pygmalien
uniqueMechanic: Economy items generate gold and interest. Buy/Sell triggers fire on merchant interactions. Max Health stacking via Lemonade Stand. Skyscraper deals damage equal to 3× your total item value.
playstyle: Pyg is the ultimate late-game tank. Sacrifice early wins to accumulate gold and scale Max Health and item value. By Day 8+, Skyscraper deals thousands of damage and your health pool is unkillable.
tips: Always buy ATM on Day 1 if offered — economy compounds every fight.
Lemonade Stand: sell small items constantly to stack Max Health.
Skyscraper's damage = 3× all your items' value. Make every slot count.
You can afford to lose early days; Prestige loss = day number, so early losses are cheap.
counters: Fast aggressive boards kill you before scaling kicks in
strongAgainst: Any late-game fight you survive to
Boards that can't burst you down quickly
lore: Boar businessman and merchant. His economy-focused personality perfectly matches his gameplay — hoard gold, convert wealth to power.

name: Dooley
uniqueMechanic: THE CORE SYSTEM: At Level 3, choose 1 of 7 Cores. Cores activate from items to the left and buff items to the right. Careful left-to-right positioning is everything. Freeze hard-counters Dooley by breaking the chain.
playstyle: Build left-to-right chains. Items on the left charge the Core; the Core amplifies items on the right. The chain can create overwhelming cascades. Dooley struggles before getting his Core at Level 3 and is vulnerable to Freeze.
tips: Hover items to see the blue glow — it shows which items interact with each other.
Fast left-neighbor items (low CD) charge the Core faster.
Freeze completely shuts down your chain — build Radiant enchantment on your Core if possible.
Chris Army Knife is a Friend + Tool + Tech in one small slot — extremely efficient.
counters: Boards that kill you before Day 3 when you get your Core
strongAgainst: Slow defensive boards that let your chain spiral out of control
lore: Small robot with grand visions of robotic freedom. Background deliberately kept mysterious by the developers.

name: Mak
tagline: Brew. Poison. Scale. The best late-game in the game.
uniqueMechanic: REAGENT SYSTEM: Reagents transform into enchanted Potions via Catalysts (Aludel, Mortar & Pestle). The transformed Potion retains the Reagent's rarity and enchantment. Mak is the ONLY hero who can apply enchantments mid-run via alchemy. Philosopher's Stone, Calcinator, and Retort gain permanent buffs with every reagent transformed.
playstyle: Survives early via regeneration, then transitions to scaling damage. Commit to one damage type (poison, burn, or weapon). The Poppy Field + Spider Mace + Floor Spike combo is considered potentially the strongest build in the entire game.
tips: Obsidian enchantment doubles damage — transforms slow poison items into powerhouses.
Poppy Field + Spider Mace + Floor Spike is potentially the best combo in the game.
Retort, Philosopher's Stone, and Calcinator stack PERMANENTLY each run — transform reagents constantly.
Mak has 'Mak-levels of regen' — you can survive early losses that would kill other heroes.
counters: High healing boards can out-regen your poison
strongAgainst: Boards that rely on healing (burn removes heal triggers)
lore: Master alchemist. Background details sparse, but his toolkit tells the story — a scientist who turns the world itself into poison.

name: Stelle
tagline: Fly. Burn. Cycle. Engines that spiral out of control.
uniqueMechanic: FLYING SYSTEM: Flying items have Slow/Freeze durations halved. Two trigger types: 'Start Flying' (triggers when item enters flying state) and 'Stop Flying' (triggers when item ceases flying). Cycling start/stop creates chain reactions. Save 24+ gold by Day 6 to hit gold-tier payoff items.
playstyle: Stack flying items for Skyliner, cycle start/stop triggers for payoffs, or run a pure Burn engine. Multiple viable archetypes with different play patterns. LavaRoller is the 'craziest Burn archetype in the game' with self-doubling capabilities.
tips: Skyliner gains +1 Multicast per flying item — stack flying items aggressively.
Hangar now gives +10 Damage and +10 Shield to ALL items baseline (even without flying).
Save 24+ gold by Day 6 — Stelle's gold-tier items are dramatically better than silver.
LavaRoller can double itself — it's the highest Burn ceiling in the entire game.
counters: Freeze halves your flying durations — still annoying even with the reduction
Boards that kill you before your engine spins up
strongAgainst: Any board that needs time to set up — your engine beats them to it
lore: Skilled mechanic and pilot who runs a workshop in the Tower District of the Bazaar. Builds contraptions that somehow always fly.

name: Jules
tagline: Celebrity chef. Converts sustain into devastating damage.
uniqueMechanic: HEATED/CHILLED SYSTEM: Stoves and Coolers are special board slots (not items) that buff Heated/Chilled keyword items. Their positions are RNG each run, making every Jules run contextually unique. CONVERSION: Durian converts Shield into Damage. Bread Knife converts Regen into Damage — pair with max health for stacking.
playstyle: Generate massive Regen and Shield through food items, then convert that sustain into burst damage via Durian (shield → damage) or Bread Knife (regen → damage). Can also run pure Burn with Microwave enabling board-wide food haste.
tips: Microwave is your engine — low CD, gives Haste to all food items.
Durian: the bigger your shield, the more damage it does. Stack shield items first.
Birthday Cake was buffed to 30% crit in Season 14 — it's now a serious pick.
Dragonmelon was moved to Gold tier in Season 14 — plan your economy around it.
counters: Boards that disrupt your Stove/Cooler positioning
strongAgainst: Boards that can't burst through your regen-stacked health
Slow burn-resistant boards that fall to your conversion damage
lore: Celebrity chef from a harsh world who now runs a restaurant at the Bazaar. Every dish is a weapon.

name: Karnok
tagline: Rage. Enrage. Hunt. The newest and most feared.
uniqueMechanic: RAGE SYSTEM: Small items generate +4 Rage, Medium +8, Large +12. At 100 Rage, Karnok Enrages — entering an empowered state. Nearly all his bronze skills trigger off Enrage. Masks (Wolf, Bear, Boar) provide direct rage. SECRET: Sled + any mask in stash = instant 100 rage, even without the mask on board.
playstyle: Tempo hero — requires early aggression. Lacks broken late-game builds, so successful runs typically finish before Day 13. Maintain gold reserves to pivot when better items appear. Hunting Knife + Hunter's Journal are mandatory early pickups.
tips: Hunting Knife + Hunter's Journal: buy these immediately. They are mandatory every single run.
Sled + any mask in stash = instant 100 Rage — even without the mask on your board.
Runic Claymore was buffed from 10% to 15% Max Health damage in Season 14.
Karnok has no overpowered late-game — plan to win before Day 13.
Jacket Exodia (Leather Jacket invulnerability) is considered near-broken when fully set up.
counters: Boards that stall into late-game where Karnok loses steam
High-regen boards that heal through your burst windows
strongAgainst: Slow-scaling heroes before they hit their power spike
lore: Hyper-aggressive hunter who can transform into a savage beast. Released March 2026 as DLC — the newest hero and the least documented on competing sites.
